quality.rationale: 需结合度规红移公式、薄壳表面应力-能量张量积分及主导能条件进行多步推导

origin.question: Problem 16.16. What is the redshift to radial infinity from a thin spherical shell in static equilibrium, in terms of its proper surface density $\Lambda_{\hat{\theta}}^{\hat{0}}$ and proper surface stresses $\Lambda_{\hat{\theta}}^{\hat{\theta}}$ ? What is the largest possible redshift if it satisfies the dominant energy condition?
origin.solution: Solution 16.16. In isotropic coordinates (see Problem 16.14), the redshift to radial infinity is$$\begin{array}{l}z = \frac {\lambda_{\text {i n f i n i t y}}}{\lambda_{\text {s u r f a c e}}} - 1 = \frac {\left(- g_{0 0}\right)_{\inf ty}^{\frac {1}{2}}}{\left(- g_{0 0}\right)_{\mathrm {S}}^{\frac {1}{2}}} - 1 = \left(\mathrm {e}^{- \gamma / 2}\right)_{\mathrm {S}} - 1 \\= \frac {1 + \mathrm {M} / 2 \mathrm {R}}{1 - \mathrm {M} / 2 \mathrm {R}} - 1 = \frac {\mathrm {M} / \mathrm {R}}{1 - \mathrm {M} / 2 \mathrm {R}}.\end{array}$$The integrated energy density and transverse pressure are respectively$$- \Lambda_{\hat {0}}^{\hat {0}} = \frac {\mathsf {M}}{4 \pi \mathsf {R}^{2}} \left(1 + \frac {\mathsf {M}}{2 \mathsf {R}}\right)^{- 3},\Lambda^{\widehat {\theta}}_{\widehat {\theta}} = \Lambda^{\widehat {\phi}}_{\widehat {\phi}} = \frac {\mathrm {M}}{8 \pi \mathrm {R}^{2}} \left(\frac {\mathrm {M} / 2 \mathrm {R}}{1 - \mathrm {M} / 2 \mathrm {R}}\right) \left(1 + \frac {\mathrm {M}}{2 \mathrm {R}}\right)^{- 3}.$$Hence the ratio of integrated pressure to energy is$$\frac {\Lambda \hat {\theta}_{\widehat {\theta}}}{- \Lambda_{\widehat {0}}^{\widehat {0}}} = \frac {\mathrm {M} / 4 \mathrm {R}}{1 - \mathrm {M} / 2 \mathrm {R}} = \frac {1}{4} zso the redshift to radial infinity is$$z = - 4 \Lambda \widehat {\theta}_{\widehat {\theta}} / \Lambda \widehat {0}_{\widehat {0}}.$$If the dominant energy condition is satisfied, $|\Lambda_{\hat{\theta}}^{\hat{\theta}_0}| \leq |\Lambda_{\hat{0}}^0|$ , so $z \leq 4$ .
